methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $6.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Drones & Autonomous. Range: $5.0B - $10.0B.

private.name: Zipline
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series G), post-money $7.6B

data.narrative:
Nuro is a Series G autonomous delivery drone company founded by Dave Ferguson and Jiajun Zhu. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of three same-sector comparable companies: Zipline (Series G, $7.6B), Joby Aviation (public, $10.0B), and Archer Aviation (public, $5.0B), spanning a $5.0B to $10.0B range.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the last post-money valuation of $6.0B and applying a typical Series G step-up multiplier of 1.25x, implies $7.5B. Peer-median analysis across the three comparables yields $7.6B, reflecting market pricing for late-stage autonomous vehicle platforms at similar scale. Both methods converge closely, supporting triangulation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $6.4B (low) to $8.7B (high), with a median point estimate of $7.5B. This represents a modest step-up from the current $6.0B post-money valuation. Key uncertainties include the timing and terms of any near-term financing event, execution risk on commercial deployment milestones, and broader market sentiment toward autonomous vehicle businesses, which remain unproven at scale. The peer set, while instructive, is small and includes both public and private companies at varying stages of commercialization.
